title: pariana obtusa
intro: Pariana obtusa, also known as the West Indian fern, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, native to the tropical forests of the Caribbean and Central America. Traditionally, Pariana obtusa has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful in treating various health issues. The plant has been associated with several health benefits, including reduced inflammation and improved wound healing, as well as potential anti-cancer properties. Pariana obtusa can be prepared as a herbal tea or infusion, and its leaves can also be used to make a poultice or applied topically to affected areas.
